According to data from the FORRT Library of Reproduction and Replication Attempts (FLoRA) there is 1 replication attempt of a finding from this study. Future research may learn from replications and build on them. Manual inspection of these articles may help to assess what led to the described outcome.

Replication article reference: Beach, S. R. H., Brody, G. H., Todorov, A. A., Gunter, T. D., & Philibert, R. A. (2011). Methylation at 5HTT Mediates the Impact of Child Sex Abuse on Women’s Antisocial Behavior: An Examination of the Iowa Adoptee Sample. Psychosomatic Medicine, 73(1), 83–87. https://doi.org/10.1097/psy.0b013e3181fdd074
Outcome: "successful"
Outcome quote: Replicating prior work, we found that a significant effect of childhood sex abuse on methylation of the 5HTT promoter region
emerged for women.
Quote source: "abstract"

Replication article reference: Salvadori, E., Blazsekova, T., Volein, A., Karap, Z., Tatone, D., Mascaro, O., & Csibra, G. (2015). PLOS ONE, 10(11), e0140570. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0140570 Outcome: "failed" Outcome quote: in our replication attempts, 9-month-olds’ preference for helpers over hin- derers did not differ significantly from chance (62.5% and 50%, respectively, in Studies 1 and 2). Quote source: "abstract" Replication article reference: Sitch, M. J. (2018). Do infants prefer prosocial others? A direct replication of Hamlin & Wynn (2011). University of British Columbia. https://doi.org/10.14288/1.0372317 Outcome: "mixed" Outcome quote: Our pre-registered sample did not replicate the original finding. However, when including all infants tested in the box scenario, the finding did indeed replicate. Quote source: "abstract" Replication article reference: Rimol, L. M., Pfuhl, G., Kjelsbøl, S. L., Fiskum, C., Wimbarti, S., Wang, W., & Johannesen, B. O. (2026). Do 9-month-old infants prefer prosocial others? Two conceptual replications and a meta-analysis of the Hamlin & Wynn box opening/closing paradigm. Child Development. https://doi.org/10.1093/chidev/aacaf052 Outcome: "failed" Outcome quote: In two independent samples of 9-month-olds, 58% of 24 Norwegian and 57% of 30 Javanese infants preferred the helper, which was not significantly different from chance. significant main effect in the full sample, the absence

of a significant main effect in this subsample, control-
ling for importance, is inconsistent with the original

actions than interpersonal actions as choices. There was
an interaction between condition and rating of the
importance of the choice, with a pattern similar to that
in the full sample. This moderation was not reported
in the original article.

Outcome quote: These findings also align with limited previous research (Rea et al., 2016; Saxey et al., 2022). Saxey et al.
